Email opens are no longer the benchmark, now it's all about conversions.

Why do you send email?

Rahul Bhavsar

This is the right shift. Opens are a weak proxy because they make teams optimize for attention instead of outcomes. For email, the useful goal is usually tied to a business action: booked call, completed onboarding step, upgrade, reply, or reactivation. I’d love to see more email tools make that conversion path visible by default.

results.title: Loops Competitors & Alternatives (2026) - Product Hunt
results.content:
If the goal is to operationalize onboarding and retention messaging with a product-minded workflow, Userlist offers a purpose-built path that can complement or replace Loops depending on how marketing-heavy the program needs to be.

### Best for

Ideal for SaaS teams running onboarding, trial conversion, and lifecycle broadcasts from a single platform.

### Standout features

 ✓Welcome and trial lifecycle automations
 ✓SaaS-focused messaging workflows
 ✓Clean, user-friendly interface
 ✓Broadcasts for product user segments

## Postmark

PostmarkPostmark

Email for web applications

5.0 · 19 reviews

Learn more → [...] The Email Platform for SaaS

5.0 · 7 reviews

Learn more →

Plunk is a strong alternative when simplicity and price matter more than an expansive lifecycle platform. Where Loops aims to cover sophisticated SaaS messaging in a polished UI, Plunk focuses on getting newsletters and basic automations running quickly with minimal setup.

Its appeal is the streamlined experience: a straightforward template editor, fast implementation, and a product that feels made for makers shipping early. That makes it a good fit for waitlists, launch updates, and lightweight customer comms that don’t require complex event pipelines. [...] Email automation for sophisticated product marketers

5.0 · 4 reviews

Learn more →

Userlist is designed around SaaS lifecycle messaging, making it a natural alternative when Loops feels either too broad or not aligned with how you run onboarding. It’s oriented toward structured customer journeys like welcome series, trial nurture, and ongoing user broadcasts—without trying to be a full ecommerce marketing suite.

Where it stands out is the focus on keeping lifecycle automation clean and approachable, so teams can build messaging flows that match product stages instead of wrestling with overly generic campaign tooling. This can be a better fit for B2B SaaS teams that want lifecycle power but prefer a calmer, less cluttered environment.

AI Data Scientist

Audience Loop is an AI workspace that turns messy contact lists into clean, enriched, always-updating audiences you can launch campaigns on. Bring in data from your CRM, events, social, Shopify, website, and spreadsheets. AI agents clean, dedupe, enrich, and match your records, then keeps audiences live and synced to Meta, Google, LinkedIn, email, TTD, and more with a built-in learning loop. No complex CDP, no data wrangling just better audiences and campaigns that perform. [...] Overview

### Are most campaigns failing because of bad creative… or bad audiences?

I ve spent years watching teams burn budget on beautiful ads aimed at garbage lists.

Same pattern every time:

 Export a target list from CRM or event platform
 Throw it into a spreadsheet
 Hack together filters, VLOOKUPs, and manual enrichment
 Pray the upload to LinkedIn/Meta/Google actually matches

results.title: Loops: Email for modern SaaS - Product Hunt
results.content:
#### What's great

I use Loops primarily to send transactional emails to my customers. Incredibly easy to set up, good developer tools (I connect seamlessly using n8n), reliable service and generous free tier for early stage start-ups. Customer service support on a Sunday - I'm impressed. Highly recommended!

#### vs Alternatives

Resend

Simpler to set up

Zachary Parker

#### What's great

Loops has a polished interface that makes email infrastructure setup intuitive.

#### What needs improvement [...] #### What needs improvement

After half a dozen outreach attempts requesting a collaborative Zoom call to explore enterprise deployment, we were declined and told our B2B logistics platform violated terms of service for 'restricted industries.' This assumption was entirely inaccurate—we operate a standard freight claims SaaS for brokers and shippers.

Actionable feedback:

Publish (more clearly) restricted industries list before onboarding

Offer discovery calls to prevent misclassification

Improve response times during evaluation (days/weeks of slow back-and-forth adds friction)

Great product for its target market, but the vetting process needs a service-level mindset. When eligibility is unclear, a 15-minute call prevents wasted cycles on both sides.

Conner Pope
